facts.text: Miami Beach’s first organized effort to shape the peninsula began in 1912 when the Miami Beach Improvement Company was founded.
In 1925 the Collins Bridge gave way to the Venetian Causeway, a series of drawbridges that still link the city to the mainland.
After decades of loss, the Miami Design Preservation League secured the Art Deco District’s place on the National Register of Historic Places in 1979.
World War II turned Miami Beach into a training hub, with the Army Air Forces Technical Training Command calling it the “most beautiful bootcamp in America.”
Miami Beach’s Atlantic coast keeps summer highs lower and lows higher than inland Florida, but its position also makes it especially prone to hurricanes.
The Miami City Ballet, founded in 1985, performs in a 63,000‑sq‑ft building near Miami Beach’s Bass Museum of Art.
